name: Time, Speed, and Distance Practice Test

questions.question: A car travels at 60 km/h for 2 hours, then at 80 km/h for 3 hours. What is the average speed?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 120+240=360 km. Time=5 hours. Avg speed=72 km/h.

questions.question: A train 120 m long crosses a pole in 12 seconds. What is its speed in km/h?
Two trains of lengths 120 m and 80 m run on parallel tracks. When running in the same direction, they take 20 seconds to pass each other. Their speeds are 54 km/h and 36 km/h. Verify the time.
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 18 km/h = 5 m/s. Time = (120+80)/5 = 40 sec. Same direction answer = 40 sec.

questions.question: If a man walks at 5 km/h, he reaches his office 6 minutes late. If he walks at 6 km/h, he reaches 6 minutes early. How far is his office?
A person covers a distance at 12 km/h and returns at 8 km/h. What is his average speed for the whole journey?
A train passes a station platform in 36 seconds and a man standing on the platform in 20 seconds. If the speed of the train is 54 km/h, what is the length of the platform?
questions.explanation: Speed = 54×5/18=15 m/s. Train length=20×15=300m. Platform+300=36×15=540. Platform=240m.

questions.question: Two trains start from opposite ends simultaneously and meet at 110 km from one end. If the total distance is 250 km, what is the ratio of their speeds?
questions.explanation: Train 1 covers 110 km, Train 2 covers 140 km. Ratio = 110:140 = 11:14.
